content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 31 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 116v–116r | **Source Images:** DEU_031_page_001.jpg, DEU_031_page_002.jpg, DEU_031_vaticanus_page_001.jpg, DEU_031_vaticanus_page_002.jpg

---

**1** Moses finished speaking all these words to all Israel, **2** and he said to them: "I am a hundred and twenty years old today. I am no longer able to lead you out and bring you in, and the LORD has told me, 'You will not cross the Jordan.' **3** The LORD your God himself will cross over ahead of you. He will destroy these nations before you, and you will take possession of their land. Joshua will lead you across, just as the LORD has promised. **4** The LORD will do to these nations what he did to Sihon and Og, the kings of the Amorites, when he destroyed them along with their land. **5** The LORD will hand them over to you, and you are to treat them exactly as I have commanded you. **6** Be strong and courageous! Do not be afraid or terrified because of them, for the LORD your God goes with you — he will never leave you or abandon you."

**7** Then Moses summoned Joshua and said to him in the sight of all Israel: "Be strong and courageous, for you will bring this people into the land that the LORD swore to their ancestors to give them, and you will settle them in as their inheritance. **8** The LORD himself goes before you and will be with you. He will never leave you or abandon you. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged."

**9** So Moses wrote down this instruction and gave it to the priests, the sons of Levi, who carried the ark of the LORD's covenant, and to all the elders of Israel. **10** Moses commanded them: "At the end of every seven years, in the year for canceling debts, during the Festival of Shelters, **11** when all Israel comes to appear before the LORD your God at the place he will choose, you must read this instruction aloud before them in their hearing. **12** Assemble the people — men, women, children, and the foreigners living in your towns — so they can hear and learn to fear the LORD your God and carefully observe every word of this instruction. **13** Their children, who do not yet know this instruction, must hear it and learn to fear the LORD your God as long as you live in the land you are crossing the Jordan to possess."

**14** The LORD said to Moses, "Your time to die is approaching. Call Joshua and present yourselves at the tent of meeting so I may commission him." So Moses and Joshua went and presented themselves at the tent of meeting.

**15** The LORD appeared at the tent in a pillar of cloud, and the pillar of cloud stood over the entrance to the tent.

**16** The LORD said to Moses: "You are about to rest with your ancestors. These people will soon begin to prostitute themselves to the foreign gods of the land they are entering. They will forsake me and break the covenant I made with them. **17** And on that day I will become furious with them and abandon them; I will hide my face from them, and they will be devoured. Many terrible disasters will come upon them, and on that day they will ask, 'Have not these disasters come on us because our God is not with us?' **18** And I will certainly hide my face on that day because of all the evil they have done in turning to other gods.

**19** "Now write down this song for yourselves and teach it to the Israelites; put it in their mouths, so that this song may be a witness for me against them. **20** When I bring them into the land flowing with milk and honey, the land I swore to give their ancestors, and when they eat their fill and grow fat and turn to other gods and worship them, rejecting me and breaking my covenant — **21** then when many disasters and terrible troubles come upon them, this song will stand as a witness against them, because it will not be forgotten by their descendants. I know what they are inclined to do, even now, before I bring them into the land I promised them."

**22** So Moses wrote down this song that same day and taught it to the Israelites.

**23** The LORD commissioned Joshua son of Nun and said, "Be strong and courageous, for you will bring the Israelites into the land I promised them, and I myself will be with you."

**24** After Moses finished writing in a scroll every word of this instruction to the very end, **25** he gave this command to the Levites who carried the ark of the LORD's covenant: **26** "Take this scroll of the instruction and place it beside the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God. Let it remain there as a witness against you. **27** For I know how rebellious and stiff-necked you are. If you have been rebellious against the LORD while I am still alive and with you, how much more will you rebel after I die! **28** Assemble before me all the elders of your tribes and all your officials, so I can speak these words in their hearing and call the heavens and the earth to testify against them. **29** For I know that after my death you will become utterly corrupt and turn from the way I have commanded you. Disaster will come upon you in the days to come, because you will do evil in the LORD's sight and provoke him to anger with what your hands have made."

**30** And Moses spoke in the hearing of the whole assembly of Israel the words of this song from beginning to end:

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Vv. 1-8 — "Be strong and courageous" (chizqu ve-imtsu):** This phrase bookends the commissioning section (vv. 6, 7, 23). The Hebrew command is emphatic and repeated — PNEUMA renders it as the rallying cry it is. The LORD's presence is the foundation of courage, not personal bravado. The double promise "never leave you or abandon you" is rendered with the same word each time to show Moses and then the LORD speaking the same assurance.

**V. 9 — "instruction" for Torah:** Throughout Deuteronomy, PNEUMA renders torah as "instruction" — the living teaching Moses has given, not merely "law" in the juridical sense. This is a body of wisdom for life in the land, not just a legal code.

**Vv. 10-13 — Hakhel (the Assembly):** The seven-year public reading of Torah during Sukkot is rendered simply as "Festival of Shelters" to keep the dynamic English flowing. The point is radical: the entire community — men, women, children, foreigners — participates. No one is excluded from hearing God's instruction.

**V. 16 — "prostitute themselves":** The Hebrew zanah (whore/prostitute after) is deliberately graphic — Israel's future infidelity to the LORD is described as sexual betrayal of covenant. PNEUMA preserves this frank language because the metaphor carries theological weight.

**V. 17 — "hide my face":** Hester panim — the withdrawal of divine presence — is rendered literally here because it is a concrete theological concept throughout the Hebrew Bible. "I will certainly hide my face" in v. 18 adds the emphatic infinitive absolute.

**V. 19 — "witness":** The song (chapter 32) is described as an 'ed — a legal witness. When Israel sins, the song will stand in court against them. This is dynamic translation of an ancient Near Eastern treaty-curse concept made accessible.

**V. 27 — "stiff-necked":** qesheh oref (hard of neck) is an idiom PNEUMA keeps in its idiomatic English form — "stiff-necked" has entered English precisely from this tradition, so it is already dynamic equivalence.

— PNEUMA
